comments.text:
Hopp Schwiz
Very impressive .. few swiss can articulate the culture so well.. but you spent too little time on the democracy to be swiss ;-D .. so very impressive research.. and contrary to almost all videos about Switzerland I've watched so far I couldn't find mistakes 
subscribed and looking forward to watching your other videos

comments.author: @PPx2517
comments.text: Swiss here. A nice very informative video. A bit overly dramatized 😅

comments.author: @jeffharris6331
comments.text: A beautiful country.  I was able to visit in the 70's and intend to go back with my wife soon.  What exactly is the economy of Switzerland?  What do they produce?  Tourism, finance, headquarters of international agencies- none of this is an actual economy.  It's great if you can pull it off, not many can.

replies.author: @kaalesrex2933
replies.text:
banking and insurance are famous of course, but they're around 10% of the economy so comparable to other european countries .. more important are pharmacuticals (23% last time I checked, but that was a long time ago ^^), precision machinery (around 20% we produce parts for precision machines, medical equipment, assembly machines, drones etc. ..) 
pretty much everything you can take some cheap resource like metal and turn it into something extremely valuable .. and that leads us to watches ^^ and tourism . both at around 12-14% .. but the economy is extremely diverse almost 90% of the economy is generated by KMUs that's to say small and medium sized buisenesses .. for example if you use a Logitech mouse or keyboard.. they're a swiss company (although sadly the quality has dwindeled a bit ever since they moved a big part of their production outside of Switzerland)

comments.author: @joanwatkins3022
comments.text: Do they have churches proclaiming the glory and salvation of God?  I didn’t think so😢

replies.author: @kaalesrex2933
replies.text:
Although we don't have a federal church, every Canton has an official believe, either catholic or swiss protestant and almost every village has a church .. and the churchbells ring every quarter hour up to 4 times and every full hour for the hour as is proper and more on Sundays and holly days. 

And they will continue to do so for all eternity .. because we know what we have and we all want to preserve it and the song of church and cow bells is the song of Switzerland

comments.author: @hermanngysel3352
comments.text: Switzerland has an important secret. The president is not elected by the Swiss people, and he is replaced every year. It is the top parties that elect him or her. Then he/she has the job for one year. The big advantage is: A fully capable person is chosen, and there are no big rallies for election where big BS is exchanged. In addition, nobody comes up with retarded promises that cost too much, or are not kept. Recent examples of that are known to everybody, the Swiss included!

comments.author: @California92122
comments.text: Well researched content and gorgeous footage. I was, however, shocked that you show "Swiss chocolate" that was not Swiss, but Polish  chocolate! Also, Zurich's Bahnhofstrasse has plenty of high-end boutiques.

replies.author: @TOURGUIDE-05
replies.text: Thanks for pointing that out! My apologies on the chocolate mix-up, good catch. And yes, Bahnhofstrasse definitely has high-end boutiques.

comments.text: Extraordinary and best ever commentary heard of the you tube video.Choice of words for narrative is of Swiss precision. I work for Swiss company here in India, this video helps to understand deeper the Swiss society and culture…

replies.author: @TOURGUIDE-05
replies.text: Glad to hear it helps you understand Swiss society and Culture better! Thanks for sharing.

comments.author: @viewfinder4488
comments.text:
As a Swiss happily married and  rising a family with 4 kids,it makes me sad to see how crazy the world has become because of greed and the crazyness of the old men's selfishness. 
Lowest crime rate, lowest traffic deaths, best public transport, almost free universities, great schools, medical services for everyone, great social security, the list goes on... 
The video reflects the truth.

replies.author: @TOURGUIDE-05
replies.text: It’s really encouraging to hear that the video accurately reflects your experience living there with your family. Thanks for sharing your perspective.

comments.author: @maliclarissaravasio8316
comments.text: What matters most is volunteer work, primarily by women and retirees. We also have the principle of solidarity in Switzerland. It's a lived reality. This means the middle class finances – and gives poorer people the right to hospital or nursing home care. There, everyone is equal, receives the same food, the same attention, and the same healthcare. That's why the rich get richer and we get poorer!

replies.author: @TOURGUIDE-05
replies.text: The solidarity principle you describe is definitely a core part of Swiss society. It’s interesting how you connect it to the wealth gap.
